target: data/sections.json part8b.html — 8b.3 section rewrite addressing SEC-8b.3.c2 major/moderate holes (dual-baseline acknowledgment, E-PRED cross-ref, Kim & Chang attribution softening, monitor.py -29 nT internal-inconsistency note)
replacement_html:
8b.3 Point 3 — Audit the eclipse stack in layers: ensemble anchor, Mohe timing, Tier 3 falsifier, and the open optics caveat.

Adopted as steel-man: we restructure our eclipse content into the four-layer presentation and publish the Tier 3 magnetic threshold explicitly. Two structural critiques survive adoption — an ensemble “anchor” fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor; and the Tier 3 magnetic Pass band itself does not distinguish dome from globe, because calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations predict the same −17 to −21 nT range.

(a) Separate the eclipse claim into four layers: (L1) ensemble anchor — WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble); (L2) Mohe timing — WIN-011 (1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse); (L3a) Tier 3 magnetic falsifier — PRED-095 (August 2026 eclipse); (L3b) Tier 3 gravity — PRED-TIER-2 (dome tier-system identifier for the 2026 eclipse gravity prediction, recycled from WIN-011; see Predictions tab); (L4) open optics caveat — the logged ray-trace gap. (b) Layered audits force explicit statements about what would falsify the claim. A falsifier that never triggers is not a falsifier; spelling out the Tier 3 threshold is due diligence.

(c) Under adoption we publish a four-row stack. L1 (WIN-068): current verdict Std Model Explains — “the Chapman mechanism (90+ years old) predicts the entire ensemble signal without dome parameters; the dome adds no derivation — only a post-hoc scaling formula calibrated against observed eclipse data.” Under adoption this is explicitly labeled a calibration layer, not an anchor. L2 (WIN-011): current verdict Not Demonstrated — the 6–7 µGal Wang signal is within 1997-era LaCoste-Romberg instrumental noise; Van Camp et al. (2001) found no eclipse gravity signal with four superconducting gravimeters (~1,000× more precise). L3a (PRED-095, magnetic): Genuinely prospective (dome registration 2026-03-12 via Git + OpenTimestamps; our tracker entry 2026-04-04; window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending). Under adoption we publish an explicit magnetic threshold — “Pass = observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone.” The dome actually publishes a dual-baseline prediction structure — BOU 2017 (per-station ∼−10 nT) and W004 empirical (per-station ∼−20 nT) — and internal code adds multipliers (KAPPA, latitude factor) producing a third value near −29 nT; our non-discrimination critique targets the W004 cluster (−17 to −21 nT, which calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits also predict), while the BOU-cluster band and the monitor.py −29 nT value are treated in Section 4.2 (dual-baseline analysis) and the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint. A Pass in this band does not distinguish dome from globe — both predict signals in this range; only the dome claims a novel derivation path, and WIN-068’s code_analysis tag (derives_from_dome:false) confirms there is no independent dome derivation to distinguish. The dome’s Pass band is moreover fitted to the same body of observed eclipse-magnetic magnitudes that L1 characterizes (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble, and the BOU 2017 single-event baseline the dome explicitly cites); the numerical specificity of the threshold is a product of shared training data, not an independent dome forecast. Note also that the dome’s own internal monitor.py produces a ∼−29 nT prediction for the August 2026 eclipse (delta_Z = −18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672, i.e., KAPPA applied on top of a BOU baseline into which KAPPA is already absorbed), which falls outside the published −17 to −21 nT Pass band — either the Pass band was set after seeing the W004-cluster numbers (goalpost-setting after data), or the dome has internal prediction heterogeneity the Pass band suppresses. See the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint for the three-formula audit. The Fail-clause concession that “signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone” would falsify the claim is in tension with the Pass criterion: Chapman’s mechanism is the SQ-dynamo, so a confirmed signal in this band is consistent with both models simultaneously, and triggering the Fail-clause would require abandoning the Pass-clause’s framing of what counts as a signal. If the dome has not pinned the threshold to this precision in its own published materials, we cite the gap as our finding. The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor) for the August 2026 eclipse — these are evaluated in Section 4.2 Eclipse and in Section 9; their status does not change the non-discrimination assessment of the published magnitude-only Pass band above. L3b (PRED-TIER-2, gravity): Recycled — restates WIN-011 with 1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse data; the falsification criterion for this layer is independent replication of the gravity signal at the 2026 eclipse sites. L4 (optics): the dome already logs the ray-trace gap; we credit the disclosure. A logged gap is not a solved gap.

(d) Adopted: layered presentation, explicit falsifier threshold. Unchanged: (i) an ensemble anchor fit to the data it “anchors” is the OTS-wrong-side pattern in physics form — the right operation applied to the wrong side; an anchor is what data hits from outside, a fit is what data draws toward itself (see the Timestamp Error tab); (ii) L3a’s Pass band is the Chapman (1933) Sq-dynamo prediction fitted to the same body of observed eclipse-magnetic magnitudes that anchors L1 (Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble and the BOU 2017 baseline the dome explicitly cites) — a Pass does not distinguish dome from globe, and there is no independent dome derivation (WIN-068 derives_from_dome:false) that would let the Pass criterion arbitrate between the two models.

ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man): four-layer eclipse stack as audit format; ensemble-anchor and L3a non-discrimination critiques unchanged.
holes_addressed: ISS-1233 (H1, major): Dual-baseline acknowledgment. Added an explicit clause inside L3a naming the BOU 2017 (~-10 nT), W004 (~-20 nT), and internal-code ~-29 nT branches, scoping our non-discrimination critique to the W004 cluster and cross-linking the BOU cluster and monitor.py -29 nT branches to Section 4.2 and the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint. (Curmudgeon originally wrote 'Section 6.2' for dual-baseline analysis — verified against sections.json that the authoritative dual-baseline treatment lives in part4 #eclipse-analysis (Section 4.2); Section 6.2 in part6 is 'The Disc Diameter,' not dual-baseline. Cross-link corrected to Section 4.2.)

ISS-1234 (H2, major): E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D cross-reference. Added a dedicated sentence in L3a naming all four sub-tests (peak-tracks-geometry, Hartland-beats-Ebro, SG 0.0 μGal, non-path stations below noise floor) with an explicit cross-link to Section 4.2 Eclipse and Section 9, scoping the non-discrimination critique to the magnitude-only Pass band and leaving E-PRED-B (the one plausibly discriminating test per the curmudgeon's stronger_arguments #2) to its primary treatment.

ISS-1235 (H3, moderate): Kim & Chang attribution softened. Replaced 'calibrated on the same Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble' with 'fitted to the same body of observed eclipse-magnetic magnitudes that L1 characterizes (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble, and the BOU 2017 single-event baseline the dome explicitly cites)' in both the L3a body and the (d) Unchanged clause. TLDR adjusted in parallel: 'calibrated on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble, also produces −17 to −21 nT' → 'calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations predict the same −17 to −21 nT range.' The attribution is now honest (Kim & Chang is named as the WIN-068 ensemble reference rather than as the dome's calibration source, since dome materials name BOU and W004 baselines and V12 H(r)/r FSF — not Kim & Chang directly).

ISS-1236 (H4, moderate): monitor.py -29 nT internal-inconsistency note added. One sentence in L3a notes the delta_Z = −18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672 formula produces ~−29 nT, falling outside the published −17 to −21 nT Pass band, with the inline annotation that this is KAPPA applied on top of a baseline into which KAPPA is already absorbed (from the WIN-025 fingerprint code-search). Deploys both the goalpost-setting and prediction-heterogeneity critiques together.
new_evidence_added: Dome raw-text/03-predictions.txt dual-baseline structure (BOU 2017 -10.9 nT | W004 empirical -22.24 nT; per-station range -5.0 nT (SNK) to -26.2 nT (HAD))

monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-025.json three-formula audit — monitor.py: -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ -29 nT; inject_ai_layer.py: -10.9 × 0.95 × 0.81 = -8.4 nT; predictions page per-station FSF 0.642-2.075
Four model-discriminating sub-tests E-PRED-A/B/C/D (raw-text/03-predictions.txt '4 Model-Discriminating Tests' listing), cross-linked to Section 4.2 and Section 9

anticipated_objections.objection: The Pass band -17 to -21 nT is our (analyst) characterization, not the dome's published range. The dome publishes per-station predictions, not a single band.
anticipated_objections.response: The -17 to -21 nT band is the W004-baseline cluster as reported in raw-text/03-predictions.txt and in data/predictions.json PRED-095. The new text acknowledges the dual-baseline structure explicitly and scopes the non-discrimination critique to this cluster, which is the concrete numerical range most frequently cited in dome materials and in our own prior text. The BOU cluster and the monitor.py -29 nT value are handled by cross-link.

anticipated_objections.objection: You say the monitor.py -29 nT falls outside the Pass band, but monitor.py is not the published prediction surface; the Pass band on predictions page IS the forward commitment. You are attacking code the author may consider exploratory.
anticipated_objections.response: We do not claim monitor.py is the forward commitment. We claim the simultaneous existence of three different dome formulas for the same event undermines the Pass band's credibility as an honest pre-registered commitment: either the Pass band was chosen after-the-fact from a menu of internal calculations (goalpost-setting), or the dome accepts internal prediction heterogeneity its public Pass band suppresses. Either way, a Pass in the published band does not validate the model's internal derivations.

anticipated_objections.objection: You cross-link to Section 4.2 and Section 9 for E-PRED handling but you do not concede E-PRED-B (Hartland > Ebro) as plausibly discriminating within 8b.3 itself.
anticipated_objections.response: The scoping choice is deliberate: 8b.3 is the Point 3 adoption rebuttal, and non-discrimination is the magnitude-only Pass band's property. E-PRED-B's relative-station-magnitude claim is a structurally different test (a relative comparison rather than a magnitude threshold). The primary treatment lives at Section 4.2 and Section 9; cross-linking there rather than duplicating inside 8b.3 keeps the section focused and honors the Part-5-is-narrative / 8b.3-is-audit division in our structure.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's dual-baseline prediction structure IS a more sophisticated commitment than a single-band threshold. Publishing two different baselines (BOU 2017 calibrated and W004 empirical) with per-station predictions and pre-registered timestamps is more than vague prospective commitment — it is a specific, numerical, site-by-site forecast. E-PRED-B in particular (HAD > EBR despite HAD having lower coverage) is a genuine relative-magnitude test that a naive Chapman+coverage-only model would predict in the opposite direction. Those are real commitments; our non-discrimination critique targets the magnitude-only Pass band precisely because the relative and geometric tests (E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D) require separate, more nuanced engagement.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: Sophisticated prediction structure does not create sophisticated prediction content when the internal derivations disagree with the published threshold. Three formulas producing -8.4, -17 to -21, and -29 nT for the same event means the Pass band is an after-the-fact selection, not a forward commitment. And the E-PRED-B relative-magnitude test, while genuinely testable, is a narrow win condition: a Pass there would still need to clear Chapman+latitude-weighted Sq-dynamo before being called dome-specific, and the magnitude-only Pass band (which is the advertised centerpiece) remains non-discriminating regardless of how the relative tests resolve.
tldr_change: Updated TLDR second sentence from 'calibrated on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble, also produces −17 to −21 nT' to 'calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations predict the same −17 to −21 nT range.' Preserves punchline, softens attribution consistent with H3.
other_side_of_the_argument: A dome defender would argue: (a) publishing dual baselines is an honest acknowledgment of model uncertainty, not prediction heterogeneity; (b) monitor.py is internal tooling not a public commitment; (c) the Pass band IS a forward commitment because it was Git+OTS-anchored 2026-03-12; (d) E-PRED-B is the real test and magnitude-only Pass is a secondary check the author concedes is indicative not conclusive. We acknowledge all four — and still hold that a magnitude band predicted by both calibrated Chapman fits and the dome-published W004 cluster is not model-discriminating, and that three different dome formulas for the same event makes the Pass band an after-the-fact selection.

scope_overstep_note: I included two small courtesy edits beyond the strict ISS scope: (a) added a parenthetical defining PRED-TIER-2 at first mention (the PRED-TIER-2 undefined-in-section minor hole from the same c2 review); (b) the registration date was already in the current sections.json text in the corrected dome-registration-2026-03-12 form (another minor hole from the same c2 review, apparently already fixed). The Fail-clause phrasing softening (also a minor from the same review) and the TLDR Chapman-direct-attribution (also a minor) were NOT touched — they are separate from the four ISS-1233..1236 items and deserve their own EXP.
